sourceMovie.title: Nefarious
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Nefarious (2023) stuck because it commits to its horror premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Cary Solomon's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The horror and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The House That Jack Built
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Nefarious clicked for you, The House That Jack Built (2018) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and drama, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Nefarious?
faq.answer: Try The House That Jack Built, The Devil's Rejects, The Exorcism of God—each shares DNA with Nefarious while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Nefarious?
faq.answer: Nefarious (2023) is best described as Horror, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Nefarious part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Nefarious Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
